content:
# Revelation Chapter 2 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 278-279 | **Source Image:** REV_002_page_001.jpg, REV_002_page_002.jpg

**1** "To the angel of the church in Ephesus, write: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven golden lampstands:

**2** I know your hard work and your endurance. I know you cannot tolerate wicked people. You have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and you have exposed them as frauds.

**3** You have endurance; you have suffered for my name and have not grown weary.

**4** But I hold this against you: you have abandoned the love you had at first.

**5** Remember how far you have fallen! Turn back and do what you did at first. If you do not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.

**6** But you have this in your favor: you hate the practices of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.

**7** Let anyone who has ears listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. To the one who overcomes, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which stands in God's paradise."

**8** "To the angel of the church in Smyrna, write: These are the words of the First and the Last, who died and came back to life:

**9** I know your suffering and your poverty -- though in fact you are rich. I know the slander of those who claim to be Jews but are not; they are a synagogue of Satan.

**10** Do not be afraid of what you are about to suffer. The devil is going to throw some of you into prison to test you, and you will face persecution for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.

**11** Let anyone who has ears listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. The one who overcomes will not be harmed by the second death."

**12** "To the angel of the church in Pergamum, write: These are the words of the one who wields the sharp, double-edged sword:

**13** I know where you live -- right where Satan has his throne. Yet you have held fast to my name and did not deny your faith in me, even in the days of Antipas, my faithful witness, who was put to death in your city, where Satan dwells.

**14** But I have a few things against you: you have people there who hold to the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to set a trap for the Israelites by luring them into eating food sacrificed to idols and into sexual immorality.

**15** In the same way, you also have people who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans.

**16** Turn back, then! Otherwise I will come to you quickly and wage war against them with the sword of my mouth.

**17** Let anyone who has ears listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. To the one who overcomes, I will give some of the hidden manna. I will also give that person a white stone with a new name written on it, known only to the one who receives it."

**18** "To the angel of the church in Thyatira, write: These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes blaze like fire and whose feet gleam like polished bronze:

**19** I know your works -- your love, your faith, your service, and your endurance -- and that your last works surpass your first.

**20** But I hold this against you: you tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. She teaches and misleads my servants into sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.

**21** I gave her time to turn back, but she refuses to repent of her immorality.

**22** Watch -- I am about to throw her onto a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into terrible suffering, unless they repent of her practices.

**23** I will strike her children dead. Then all the churches will know that I am the one who searches minds and hearts, and I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.

**24** But to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not hold to her teaching and have not explored what they call 'the deep secrets of Satan' -- I will place no other burden on you.

**25** Only hold on to what you have until I come.

**26** To the one who overcomes and keeps doing my works until the end, I will give authority over the nations --

**27** that person will rule them with an iron rod, shattering them like clay pots --

**28** just as I received authority from my Father. And I will give that person the morning star.

**29** Let anyone who has ears listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

Chapter 2 contains four of the seven letters to the churches. Key dynamic choices:

- **"Exposed them as frauds"** (v. 2): The Greek ΕΥΡΕΣ ΑΥΤΟΥΣ ΨΕΥΔΕΙΣ literally means "found them false." The dynamic rendering captures the investigative and unmasking quality of the Ephesian community's discernment.

- **"Remember how far you have fallen"** (v. 5): The Greek ΠΟΘΕΝ ΠΕΠΤΩΚΑΣ ("from where you have fallen") implies altitude and distance. This rendering emphasizes the dramatic spiritual descent rather than a simple "remember from where you fell."

- **"Synagogue of Satan"** (v. 9): I retained this stark phrase from the manuscript. It reflects intra-Jewish conflict in the first century, not a blanket condemnation. The Greek ΣΥΝΑΓΩΓΗ ΤΟΥ ΣΑΤΑΝΑ describes a specific community making false claims to identity, not Judaism broadly.

- **"Right where Satan has his throne"** (v. 13): Pergamum housed major imperial cult temples and the great altar of Zeus. The language is vivid and spatial -- the church lives in the very seat of hostile power. The Sinaiticus text clearly reads ΘΡΟΝΟΣ ΤΟΥ ΣΑΤΑΝΑ.

- **"Set a trap"** (v. 14): The Greek ΒΑΛΕΙΝ ΣΚΑΝΔΑΛΟΝ means literally "to throw a stumbling block." A "trap" conveys the predatory, intentional nature of the deception that Balaam taught.

- **"The deep secrets of Satan"** (v. 24): ΤΑ ΒΑΘΕΑ ΤΟΥ ΣΑΤΑΝΑ -- this appears to be the group's own language turned against them. They likely claimed to know "the deep things" (of God), but Christ reframes their esoteric knowledge as satanic. The scare quotes around the phrase signal this ironic reframing.

- **"Morning star"** (v. 28): ΤΟΝ ΑΣΤΕΡΑ ΤΟΝ ΠΡΩΙΝΟΝ -- a promise of eschatological glory and participation in Christ's own identity (cf. Rev 22:16). I keep the image vivid rather than explaining it away.

-- PNEUMA
